name: Sotalbo and Manqueospese Castle Loop
name_original: Cuesta del Chicho y Castillo de Manqueospese — circular desde Sotalbo - Espacio protegido Sierras Paramera y Serrota
description: This difficult 8.1-mile (13.0 km) jogging route from Sotalbo leads to the historic Castillo de Manqueospese with panoramic views.
region_intro: Jogging around Sotalbo offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by river valleys and undulating terrain. The region features routes that traverse open countryside and lead to historical sites. Elevation changes vary, with some paths following river courses while others ascend to higher viewpoints. Best jogging routes around Sotalbo The most popular jogging route is Sotalbo and Manqueospese Castle Loop, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the local landscape and passes a historic castle. Another top favourite among local runners is Adaja River Trail loop from El Fresno, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This route follows the Adaja River, offering a flatter running experience. Local runners also love the Pico Zapatero – Molino de Enmedio loop from Sotalbo, a 11.8 miles (19.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Jogging around Sotalbo is defined by river trails, open countryside, and routes with notable elevation changes, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Sotalbo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Sotalbo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sotalbo?
A: There are over 18 running routes around Sotalbo, offering a diverse range of experiences for runners of all levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Sotalbo?
A: Yes, Sotalbo offers some easier options. For a moderate, flatter experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adaja River Trail loop from El Fresno&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the river and has minimal elevation gain. There is also a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31474607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Duruelo&lt;/a&gt; which is moderate and shorter.

Q: Where can I find challenging running trails around Sotalbo?
A: For a more challenging run, Sotalbo has several options with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero – Molino de Enmedio loop from Sotalbo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile trail with substantial climbs. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159440123/sotalbo-and-manqueospese-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotalbo and Manqueospese Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.1 miles and also rated difficult.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Sotalbo?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sotalbo are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adaja River Trail loop from El Fresno&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero – Molino de Enmedio loop from Sotalbo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Sotalbo's running trails?
A: The running trails in Sotalbo offer a diverse landscape, characterized by river valleys and undulating terrain. You'll find paths traversing open countryside, some following river courses, and others ascending to higher viewpoints with notable elevation gains. This variety provides options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What interesting sights can I see while running in Sotalbo?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159440123/sotalbo-and-manqueospese-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotalbo and Manqueospese Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the local landscape and passes a historic castle. You might also encounter natural landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt; or various mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Navalmoral&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there running routes in Sotalbo with good viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region features paths that ascend to higher viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31501699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebrera – Mirador Estelar El Barraco loop from El Barraco&lt;/a&gt; includes a section near the Mirador Estelar, offering panoramic views. Routes around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt; also provide excellent vistas.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Sotalbo?
A: The running routes in Sotalbo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from river trails to challenging ascents, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there options for trail running in Sotalbo?
A: Yes, Sotalbo is well-suited for trail running, with many routes traversing varied terrain and offering significant elevation changes. The region's mix of open countryside and undulating paths provides an excellent environment for trail runners seeking a challenge.

Q: Are the running trails in Sotalbo dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, many trails in the Sotalbo countryside are suitable for running with dogs, especially if they are kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage and be mindful of livestock or wildlife in agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any shorter running routes, for example, around 5k, in Sotalbo?
A: While many routes are longer, you can find shorter options or adapt existing ones. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31474607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Duruelo&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 6.3 kilometers (just under 4 miles), making it a good choice for a quicker run.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails in Sotalbo?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the villages where many routes begin, such as Sotalbo itself or El Fresno. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas, especially for popular trailheads.
tips: The Río de la Garaganta descends through this valley that has formed over the years and that lets us see how the Sierra de Panamera peaks rise behind us.

Manqueospese Castle, also known as "Althoughospese," is located in the municipality of Mironcillo, Ávila, at an altitude of 1,360 meters, overlooking the Amblés Valley. This castle is an example of a European mountain fortress, with strong walls adapted to the rocky terrain.

Construction of the castle began in the 15th century by the Dávila family and was completed by Esteban Dávila in the 16th century. The castle is surrounded by legends, including stories of unrequited love and rivalries between knights. Its name, "Manqueospese," is said to come from the expression "although it weighs on you," which is related to these legends.
From its privileged position, the castle offers stunning views of the Amblés Valley and the walls of Ávila. Although it is currently in ruins, restoration and cleaning work are underway.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this circular route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult jogging route, best suited for experienced runners or hikers with good physical fitness. The terrain involves a significant ascent, starting as a sand track from Sotalbo, then becoming a path through rocky sections and scrubland. Near Manqueospese Castle, expect sections with stones that require careful footing.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this 13 km circular route with over 520m of elevation gain typically takes approximately 1 hour and 52 minutes. However, this duration assumes a steady jogging pace and does not account for stops to enjoy the views or explore the castle.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Manqueospese Castle, a 15th-century fortress offering extensive panoramic views of the Amblés Valley and the Sierra de Yemas. You'll also pass by Alto Cuesta del Chicho (Sierra de la Paramera), providing impressive vistas as you ascend through the protected natural space of Sierras Paramera y Serrota.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cuesta del Chicho y Castillo de Manqueospese circular trail?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Sotalbo. You should be able to find parking within the village to begin your journey.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: No, this specific circular route is rated as difficult and requires very good physical condition due to its significant ascent and rocky sections. It is not recommended for beginners, young children, or those seeking a leisurely family stroll. There is a shorter, easier hiking route to the castle from Sotalbo (3 km, 184m elevation gain, 1-1.5 hours) if you are looking for a less demanding option.

faqs.question: Is the Cuesta del Chicho y Castillo de Manqueospese trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the protected area generally allows dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain, potential wildlife, and the presence of livestock. Ensure your dog is also fit enough for a difficult, rocky, and lengthy route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for this route, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for jogging. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring snow or icy conditions, making the already challenging terrain even more difficult and potentially hazardous.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the protected area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Cuesta del Chicho y Castillo de Manqueospese trail or the protected natural space of Sierras Paramera y Serrota.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the protected Sierras Paramera y Serrota, characterized by scrubland, rockrose, and broom. While specific wildlife sightings can vary, you might encounter typical Iberian mountain fauna. The landscape is dominated by granite formations, especially around Manqueospese Castle, which is built upon a granite promontory.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation in Sotalbo?
faqs.answer: Sotalbo is a small village, and while it may have basic amenities, it's advisable to check in advance for specific cafes, pubs, or accommodation options, especially if you plan to stay overnight or need refreshments immediately after your run.

faqs.question: Is there a less challenging option to visit the Castillo de Manqueospese?
faqs.answer: Yes, if the full circular route is too demanding, there is a shorter, easier hiking route to Manqueospese Castle directly from Sotalbo. This alternative is approximately 3 km long with 184m of elevation gain and typically takes 1 to 1.5 hours to complete.
